figures.caption: Figure 4: Precision-Recall curves for Few-Shot synthetic data augmentation experiment models.
Figure 5: Flow for Zero-Shot Synthetic Data Generation: Capturing defect embeddings from 4 Product A samples to synthesize 150 high-fidelity defects directly onto Product B surfaces.
Figure 6: Precision-Recall curves for Zero-Shot detection experiment models.
Figure 1: The three-stage generative pipeline: Embedding Learning, Conditioned Generation, and Seamless Integration.

Figure 2: Qualitative results across diverse industrial surfaces. Left: One real example defect image used in the embedding learning phase. All 3-5 sample defect images used in embedding learning are structurally similar. Middle (In-Domain): Synthesized defects generated on the instances of the native source material. Right (Cross-Domain): Zero-shot transfer of the learned defects onto completely different, unseen material textures, demonstrating successful disentanglement of the defect from its original background. Real examples and the background images are from MVTec AD dataset [3]
Figure 3: Flow for Few-Shot Synthetic Data Generation: Learning a defect embedding from 4 real Product B samples to generate 150 diverse synthetic variations on the same product surface.
